decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
royal paps integ

diamond-legend

Real winrate is a bit higher because this includes a slightly worse build, probably take away like 1-2 losses





I'll actually provide strategy tips on this since it's clearly in a category of decks that I call "competent meme"

1.Do not play this deck. It's incredibly clunky and the optimal way to play it is probably focus less on royal paps and add a bit more proactive and/or cheaper drops into this. The deck also can use Golden Hits. However my aim was to make an rpaps deck.

2.Always mulligan for Fortune. Your second priority mulligan is Onionsan since it's your only proactive drop (bar Undyne).

3.You can skip turn3 as player 2 as long as you think EX isn't coming. This will let you play big threat t4 + follow up swing t5. If you play big threat t3, opponent responds, you can be forced to skip t4 and you or your remaining board will take damage from the responding card.

4.Ex is nasty but not the end of the world if the enemy cannot pop your gifts. Gifts often let you come back from an ex since you get 3 additional gold. Yes, 3, because integrity passive will count the gold ex stole from you as "spent" and the passive will trigger even if you don't actually fulfill it after the ex.

5.Count your carousels. Without one, Asgore can easily turn into a dead card, and since this deck has no cycle, you really don't want dead cards. Mad Dummy can help as a last resort there. It's actually the weakest card in the deck, but I still prefer it over Jevil, though whether that's right of me is very arguable.

6.Contrary to popular belief, if you use Ugly Fish on a monster that costs less than 28, your ass will not spontaneously light on fire and kill you. Please.

7.Always think at least 1 turn ahead.

decks.author: mark_callisto
decks.notes:
Overgrowth-Mid-Bravery
36/6 aka 86% wr (tho real wr is higher because multiple accounts, source: trust me bro)
Played to 2280

A combination of my recent dabblings into overgrowth and old fishvery

Gameplan: you have the tools to consistently win earlygame exchanges and snowball them into a midgame victory. Some common openings with this deck are recruit drop into rage, froggit trio overgrowth (hitting mettabot and recruit pick as well), berd/clam girl and in case of a big enemy opener mettabot + susie or ugly fish. During mulligan you should keep these in mind and pick and choose depending on the matchup. Duck off (and by extension noelle) and heroine serve as your way to close the game out, keep in mind that you don’t have burst outside of random recruit drops so you gotta hit face whenever possible. 

Red bird is the 25th slot here but definitely worth it because it plays off your opponents perception of threat, allowing you to get enormous value from it (for example an opponent is very likely to ignore an ugly fish or damaged broken car in favor of building a board)

6/10 braincells required, mainly good mulligan and trading awareness

decks.author: dylanhall
decks.notes:
"Merc-Hire-Madness"

This is merc hire! Do you miss old integ with sillystrings spam and 23 good cards being viable? NOW YOU CAN DO THAT AND MORE!!!! 22 good cards and 1 dead card equal a whole fun value control stall mish mash of wacky stuff.

18-2

This deck was changed once.

2x Carn tent -> 1x Carn tent + Captn Rouxls

2x Polibear -> 2x Tall Sink (Yes, actually)

How to play:

Play control value integ. Try and play on curve if you can on t3-4 and then try and just go into control mode and keep doing the most value based lines (unless your setting up to die or are gonna die). Bookshelf is usually a dead card but then again, you only need 1 if you want near infinate value. Carn tent is... bad. So just dream it away so we never need to look at this atrocity again.

Played from D2 -> ONYX 1 (so close)

The combo: 

Have either a full board or 1 other monster on the far left.

Bookshelf -> Merc hire -> Other monster far left -> Carn Tent

"But im dying to Everyman Eye!!!!"

Simple: Get 2 shelfs and Tent

Play Shelf -> Cloning -> Clone shelf -> Play 2nd Shelf -> Merc hire -> Carn tent!

You now have 3 one cost shelfs and a 1 cost tent. (Mill is pretty irrelevant at this point)

Next turn:

Shelf×2 -> Silly Strings -> Shelf-> Merc hire -> Tent

You have now, Killed 2 monsters outright, created a board, presented and infinate loop, and gained +2 Anti.

You can get board locked but you still have 13 DMG which is a clock, not fast but they do need to answer it. 

Loses:

- Good tempo kindness where I died to not enough healing vs 12 burst. (The game made me change the deck up)

- Nearly perfect Libness draws and they never even played a lib. So I lost cuz insane tempo and always being behind from t3 onwards.
You can usually stabilize most the time but kindness is quite hard to

I really liked going back to a kind of old integ with ridiculous value and loops for random BS.

But this will need to be changed. This is dumb and way to easy to abuse lol.

decks.author: 4reis
decks.notes:
acidic-casdyne-giga-otk 
(original deck by <@971207396183650354> but i tried to find a way to adapt/counter to ⁠six-hp-lol users)

As I am writing this, I am in Legend with 2255 elo. #2 currently. 

I have played nothing but patience giga queen so far in ranked.

My winrate is a 70% right now because I was playing previous versions of this deck.

Giga Queen deck nerf please I beg. It's so fun to play otk vs stall but this is straight up unbalanced. -30 health in a singular turn with Casual Undyne hitting once and then using two Giga Attacks afterwards?

For players trying to read this and have no experience whatsoever playing Giga Queen, she requires 7 TOKENcards, which I will be calling them as Green Cards because it's much simpler to identify something by the color instead of using the word "Token". This is due to the stereotype that your average Undercards player cannot read huge walls of text.

Playstyle is dependent on matchup. Please do not take Giga Queen, Casual Undyne or Receptionist 3 as part of the 3 cards in your starting hand. It is never a good idea to have heavy cards in your starting hand versus aggro/zoo, you'd want them later. 

Try to get Giga Queen before turn 10 though when you've played 5 other green cards for it. 

Your deck does not need to calculate 7 green cards, but 5 instead because:

1. You will be playing this deck in Patience. Change of Winds is a green card.

2. You have Ambition as one of your artifacts. Dream is a green card.

7-2 is 5. I'm sure by now if you're reading this you know what basic math is.

The level of this deck is Intermediate-Advanced. 


Quality of Life Change for lazy people:


**Great cards to have before and during turn 5:**
**Tem Egg, Maus, Bag of Goodies, Memorial Statue, Purple Mascot, Cracked Table, Policeblook, Rudolph, Jar of Worms, Traffic Light.**

**Cards you would want by turn 10:**
**Giga Queen, Casual Undyne, Receptionist 3** (if you want).

**Tips for Traffic Light:**
Please remove **Change of Winds ** from your **Patience** passive & **Dream** from your **Ambition** artifact before playing it so then your **Traffic Light **targets the green card in your hand. **Mines & Hyperlink** decks will be countering your **Traffic Light** as they are both green cards. 

**Tips for the OTK (one turn kill):**
Your opponent should have** 1-4 monsters** when you play down **Casdyne** (short for **Casual Undyne**). Please make opposing monsters **1-5 health** by then. You can buff your **Casdyne** with the **Kr effect from Purple Mascot**, **Shield from Bag of Goodies**, and the **Tea Set from Cracked Table** before attacking. 

This is before you place down **Giga Queen**.
If the monsters have 3 or less health, it is optional to use **Purple Mascot, Gemstones and Prison Bar** to lower them down to 1 health.

This is after you place down **Giga Queen**.
If the monsters have 4 health, use **Giga Glass**. 
If the monsters have 5 health, use **Giga Balls**.

**VERY IMPORTANT: You prefer to have 3 monsters on the opponent side.**

To do the **OTK** (very situational, dependent on opponent's health, and let us say that the monsters have 1 health), total means the max amount of damage you can do without prior buffs: 

A regular **Casdyne** has 8 attack, 9 health and has **Haste**. The casts of **Giga Kick** and **Giga Punch** is targeted on **Casdyne**.

A **Giga Punch** on **Casdyne** makes it a 11 attack monster if there are no other buffs on her. 
Steps when it states **(Alternate Step)** go from 
**3.5** -> **4.5** 
not **3.5** -> 4

Enemy has 1 monster.
1. Play**Casdyne**
2. **Giga Queen**
3.  Attack with **Casdyne** (7 damage) / cast **Giga Kick** (14 damage) / cast **Giga Punch** (10 damage)

Enemy has 2 monsters (Perhaps you played **Receptionist 3** that same turn).
1. Play**Casdyne**
2. **Receptionist 3** (maybe to get the other monster out, would be used if there isn't 2 monsters total)
3. Attack lowest hp enemy monster with your **Casdyne** (7 damage)
**3.5. (Alternate Step)** Play **Giga Queen** to then cast **Giga Punch** (10 damage)
4.  Play **Giga Queen** to cast **Giga Punch** (17 damage total)/ **Giga Kick** (21 damage total)
**4.5. (Alternate Step) Giga Kick** after **Giga Punch**(30 damage total)

Enemy has 3 monsters (Perhaps you played **Receptionist 3** that same turn).
1. Play**Casdyne**
2. **Receptionist 3** (maybe to get the other monster out, would be used if there isn't 3 monsters total)
3. Attack lowest hp enemy monster with your **Casdyne** (7 damage)
4. Play **Giga Queen** to cast **Giga Punch** (17 damage total)
5. Cast **Giga Kick** (37 damage total)

This is without prior buffs, such as **Shield from Bag of Goodies(+1 attack per cast of Shield), Kr from Purple Mascot (+1 attack per monster Casdyne attacks and kills depending on board, max would be +3 attack), and Tea Set from Cracked Table (+1 attack per cast of Tea Set, max 2 attack)**

decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
berdly statue ultima

...featuring heal because i am beyond even professional help at this point
----
Step 1) Shit out tokens. Octofriend is good. Recep1 is your earlygame monster that's just sorta existing so you don't mill and you have something to play meaningful octofriends on. Ferry is worse because that 5th atk point matters.

Step 2) Shit out Berdly Statues. If you don't have Berdly Statues, you probably have draw. Draw Berdly Statues to shit out.

Step 3) Win the game.
----
- Heal should mostly be used as a cycle on face and means to stall out a turn or two vs bursty decks or turns 12-15 vs midrange board. That said, this is a 60/40 "mostly", not a 90/10 "mostly", but the situations where you use heal to remove a silence or debuff will come naturally. Don't hold it.

- For people who are unfamiliar with ultima: when it yoinks a card from your hand to shove its insides there instead, the card it yoinked **goes to the top of your deck.** Always. That means you can accurately predict your draw on every odd turn. For example if your turn 2 draw was Maus but it got replaced by Laser Topper, your turn 3 draw is always gonna be Maus. **Use that to your advantage.**

- Since Ultimate Heal can't draw spells, it has a heightened chance of getting you a Cell Key after you've used the Broken Key. Play around that and use your other draw in according order.

- This deck has a deceptively high amount of draw. You can totally draw your deck by t15, but this also means you're not winning any fatigue wars, and this deck doesn't even have Snoelle. Don't be passive vs stall. If you have to be passive, don't ever waste your ex. Hold it till ultima unless you have to prevent something like a Snowgrave.

- You don't have that many spells, and Head Hathy is pretty cheap. Try to play her right after you've used a spell - you can hold the Breaking Love. Note that this doesn't apply to Caged Kings, since they're much more expensive.







Creator: <@519567102781423630>

Some more stuff about the deck:

- You can beat GIGA Queen via Toriel + Round Devices next turn if they waste their glass. It's hilarious.

- If you don't have something from this deck, the replacement is always Susie.

- This deck is prone to self-locking in the earlygame. Spread your tentacles evenly to make sure your board actually has somewhat meaningful damage and you don't end up with Maus-Boxed Body-Left Tentacle-Mobility Tracks.

- You can use Round Devices + Test of Will on boards that are normally immune to it. You can also use the inverse combination and a bit of other damage to set GIGA Queen to 1 ATK if you didn't draw Toriel.
Also, start going face whenever given the opportunity around turn 11. Most players will give you this opportunity. Good players, if they do, will try to facerace you, but then you show them Heal and they wonder what mental asylum you crawled out of before you blast them 12 to 0 with gambit

decks.author: dylanhall
decks.notes:
Elite-Shelf-Teg 
(Onyx IV - Master III | 15-5 | 75% WR)

An old deck I always like to make when im bored, this is Shelf-Teg. Its a weird Midrange Control deck, using early game tempo from Police/Stickman/Snake Miner/Poli Bear with the combination of Elite Soldiers and other good cards to try and out tempo early game, and out stall late game.

Integ Bookshelf is the name sake due to the fact it has great options for most situations. Silly Strings for Stall, Merc Hire for Mirrors, and Fortune for if you want to play a 3/4/6 with passive or just need a card out of hand. The reason the deck is built so low to the ground compared to other decks like this is so you can nearly always get Shelf from Elite Soldiers, allowing for better stall and 2/5/7s! (it works trust me....)

Play an early drop so you get T4 Elite Soldiers, mabye get a 3/7/4 for your Poli Bear, who knows.

Pretty fun deck, mostly a pet card deck. Play at your own risk, I know people will play this wrong as say bad deck <:hue:1137287556757721178>

coffinteg
13-2, played to onyx 1 season start
i had a game with sktima that was likely to be lost (but idk for sure) but it softlocked. was before i decided to run 2x lasso though

generally this deck tends to fold to either burn-y strategies or a bad hand. (actually, both losses here are to a chujin, so keep that in mind.) i generally would advise not playing coffin without a way to dupe or kill it, because most decks have silence or some other tech, and it will entirely remove your big guy from the dustpile. however, if you are facing a deck you know wont have an answer, go ham

beyond combo management, the rest of this deck is just good tempo plays and resource management

and remember that coffin doesnt have non-DT so it CAN target chara >:). other important notes: chujin tombstone only takes non-generated monsters out of dustpile, but it does remove them, so it can clear your dustpile for another round of coffins. coffin also only targets distinct non-coffin monsters, and it can target generated ones, too

actually in retrospect, maybe this deck should run an investment or two. feel free to slot something out for it, and if you find success with that, tell me which cards you ended up removing

decks.author: iamoseedee
decks.notes:
Name: kbo-chargestice

Description: This iteration of Aggro Justice notably excludes Temmie Eggs, Vasebreakers, Small Birds, and Ceroba. It alternatively has Flies, Headshots, and most importantly: Ball Dancers. With a greater than 70% WR according to the most recent record of 14 wins out of 19 games, I woke up a lowly DIAMOND but will sleep a LEGEND.

Gameplan:
- Ideally, mulligan anything with Charge to play Ball Dancer(s) ASAP. It is possible to play one on T2 (2+1+2) or two by T4 (1+1+2+3+4). Remember that Ball Dancers also buff other Ball Dancers, so playing the first two would require a total of 11G.
- Be mindful of when you might need that extra DMG or G. Consider whether using multiple Duals or Blitz Quizzes now will save the pricier Silence of Headshot for when you need it more, for example.
- Treat the endgame as an OTK. You likely have a larger HP pool, maybe one or two Flying Heads with Taunt, or unused spells. These will allow you to save more of your Gold for Chujin and any monsters in your hand to secure the kill without worrying about drawing a Temmie Egg or Vasebreaker when you need a Charge monster, more DMG, or empty board slots.

decks.author: icenovice4163
decks.notes:
tri-rare tempo
17-5 from ruby 4 to onyx 4 (top 10)
hadn't read the posting rules yet so i forgot to take a screenshot of the rank before switching decks but i remember reaching 10th place

it's called tri-rare because of the three rare need monsters: sushipants, berdly plush, and first starwalker. i originally made this based on a funny idea i had of fwalker -> dummy x2 -> el bailador. el bailador and madjick are actually really good here and you can close out matches quite abruptly that way, but you can also just farm the longevity (not the spell) of your monsters and you'll probably hold enough tempo to win. i actually started playing this yesterday but the deck changed quite a bit overnight, with me adding scarf mouse, cracked table, and jevil this morning. dummy is your taunt (have faith in the little guy), mettaton is shockingly dangerous with stat buffs (especially at +2/+2), scarf mouse is (obviously) your silence card, sushipants holds bigger monsters in check, madjick is surprisingly good removal, especially with sushipants and buffed dummies, cracked table is an incredible card for tempo, grillby closes 1 dmg gaps while keeping tempo, berdly plush has decent stats while letting you curve out higher-value monsters, alphys... didn't really end up doing anything valuable, bookshelf is a metaphorical swiss army knife with test of skill, soothing, ultimaheal, and especially pie, jevil is mostly useful for diamond chaos and devilsknife, and fwalker is your primary opener. with the buff to 6 hp, fwalker is actually able to somewhat keep tempo now. it's the lowest-tempo card in the deck (aside from alphys), so the buff is a huge boost and was a big part of this deck's success.

decks.author: xcriwn
decks.notes:
***MaxHPNess (elite soldiers version)***

15 - 5, played in master to legend (75% win rate)
There are a lot of things to say so lets divide them into sections:

**Early Game**

In early game you want to see the opponents' soul and artifacts and get an idea of wether they're gonna rush you or not. For games against rush, you want to keep any healing, for stall games you can mulligan for other things like chara, loren (for the 3 dmg) or evil eye. 

When you're not being chargespam-rushed, you want to keep chara in hand every time. (Chara has a habit of always botdecking so get used to it not being that present). 

You want to generally start with an innkeeper or evil eye if you have it and have it die by turn 4 so you get it back with elite soldiers (this depends though, if doing that means you're milling it may be best to just not let it die so early).

This deck absolutely crushes any chargespam so if you hate it, you're in luck.

**Mid Game**

The mid game consists on activating your combos. Your combos generally consist of: papyrus + pancakes, some kinda bookshelf combo or even cardmaster if opponent is stalling out. 

Papyrus is a really strong card for this deck, especially when healed by pancakes. 

You also must learn when to just accept you're gonna mill and just save g, milling is going to happen 100% of the time so you have to know what you're saving for before milling.

**Late Game**

You'll reach late game with mostly stall-ish decks, so i threw in a dt extractor (accompanied sometimes by reporter mtt) to help with that. 

The combinations of cardmaster + bookshelf and others will keep you alive.

**Other Tips**

You can also lock opp's board and stall the fuck out, then heal and cardmaster.

G is important af, you can always heal so dont mind taking damage.

Solid Dawg 
(Annoying dog, solidity, Berdly statue) 

14-4 (from as far as i can read) 11 most recent games in legend and the rest in master 



The general idea is to combine two strong things: Bravery's overgrowth and token drops; and berdly statue + solidity (and chutomb)

Annoying dog, Assault, overgrowth, knight's shield and Rorrim the early game. 

3 accels is necessary to dig for rorrim, annoying dog or just draw the shields without having to pay 3g, i think with 3 assaults, the draw is rarely ever overwhelming. The deck also has quite a few expensive cards so having accel to erase them is nice. 

Berdly statue + chutomb is the pay out, Ice - E can be both a finisher, or a set up for a finisher turn (the ice can be used to lock an opponent down alongside snoelle so you can restraint them and click face the next turn) 

Omega flowey is a really strong finisher, especially with the ice locking the opponent down. 

Snoelle cuz everything she does is helpful for this deck, the para helps with the aforementioned lock, snowgrave do provide a extra punch to finish an opponent off, ice crystal also has taunt so they become "anti fatigue" in the very end. (oh yeah, and she becomes more consistently drawn after accel and overgrowth runs through most of the deck)

oh yeah, two patience games in the middle cuz i got bored and i wanted something to really kill charge spam 

The worst matchup this deck has faced throughout this season is vet midrange:  vet kindness or vet pv. Spade chain just turn rorrim into a "you wasted 7g" card and generally the deck doesn't have a good tool for picking off 1-2 hp targets after they traded, besides assault or recruit drop. And while ice - e does slow the pace of the match down, as well as the dog residues. I often end up too battered by the time i get to berdly statue and just die (or die before that) 

Spaming dog residue and annoying dog also means the deck dies to tasque singer.

decks.author: dylanhall
decks.notes:
Power-Totem

(LEGEND 2160~ -> LEGEND 2303 (6th Place, Feb 27th) | 26/3 | ~90% WR)

History has the first 4 games use the current list with Kris, 2x Fortune Tellers. These were traded for Sneo, 2x Forest Worm.

This deck wants to play 2x Totem, 3x Powerhouse, and see if it cant get that slow encroaching Tempo build up at the cost of early game tempo.

Loses were 2 games vs "Its Toilet, but I also lay 4x lasso Tasque Singer twice a game" could have won the last game if my totems were not some of the last cards in my deck, they had toilet, any taunt, Susie, Coffee would have won me a turn to draw 2nd totem, or the game. 

I was trying to play too defensively the first game vs max and then payed the price. Learned to be more tempo forward and to simply play as if they always had Chujin, even though they didn't.

Most notable interactions and tips:

Swatch, if possible hold swatch until you can trigger him off Powerhouse at least once. this can be done on t4, play Powerhouse + Swatch, you spend 10g, and if you have any S-Birds or Pink Lasers, you can usually play one of them as well to avoid milling.

It is ok to sometimes play just a 4/4/7 Pink Laser, even 4/3/7. You might just need the early board control and 3/7 is hell of a rate for 4.

You actually have a lot more Burst then you would think. Totem has an almost 70% chance to give you a card that will give you 3 burst the turn of, or after. I have won some games by taking MTT Reporter knowing I had a Copdyne in my deck to Bounce, and getting the extra burst.

People are very bad at playing around Sneo. It has actually been shocking at how often I see a tempo/midrange deck give me 32 HP and then try and Face Race me.

I wish I didn't need to say this, but keep track of your Totem count (Dustpile and potential on board and in hand, how many turns it will take for them to die, etc.), and your Glam Triggers. These are very important to play around for maximum Tempo.

Deck is very simple at base but has a lot of pilot skill checks for some simple things, 6/10 Braincells needed.

decks.author: Deleted User
decks.notes:
The Dojo Special - 18/25 | 72% Winrate - Played from Master III -> Legend (2125 -> 2205)
(It isn't shown here but a win against Casdyne as well)


Mid-Range Kindness deck that operates using Elite Soldiers to refuel, Elite Soldiers is your friend and don't be afraid to mill / sacrifice your monsters (especially early for the T4 proc) if it means you get valuable monsters back. You'll want to aim to secure the board and play on tempo, remove opponents stuff and get good trades in. If your opponent has a plan to win that you very unlikely can't come back from (Especially if it's soon), make sure to race ASAP but don't completely forget about board. Make sure to aim for the Turn 4 Elite Soldiers proc as much as you can (when it's safe to) as that is a HUGE boon that can mean you just win the game from there. Also make sure to keep the pedal to the metal as slowing down can ensure you fall behind and lose.

I encourage you all to make your own spin on this deck and mix and match, if there are cards you don't like in here, throw in something you do like!


General Mulligan Rules:
**Almost always** keep Spider in your hand for a near guaranteed T4 Elite Soldiers proc
Chargespam - Hard mulligan for Anti-aggro / removal
Aggro/Zoo - Hard mulligan for Counters / keep One 4-6 cost proactive if you already have 2 Counters
Mid-range - Keep One 4-6 cost proactive and 1 removal, 3rd option depends
Control - Keep Two 4-6 cost proactive and find a removal
Stall - Hard mulligan for only 4-6 cost proactive (except Spider)

**__Some recommended cards you can swap in!__**

**Silence / Counters**
2x Memorial Statue - 1x in already - Run two if you REALLY need more silence
1x Jerry - Better generally, not as potent anti-aggro
1x Piranhas - Consistent 4 DMG removal that lives as a 4/4
1x Mister Ruckus - Consistent removal while being a Taunt

**Long Game**
1x Chujin Tombstone - Tested minorly, can help immensely with refueling (if you don't want to use Soulless Kris) and give you an extra push
1x Cyberdly - At max you can get 3 Thrashing Machine which can prove handy for removal / gaining value
1x Lancer - Very potent removal that can get you out of tricky spots, can be hard to find the handspace for both Spades early-game

**Beatstick**
1x Bloody Tree - Classic beater, absurd T2, falls off later in the game
2x Donation Stump - 1x in already - Can't scale like Bloody Tree, instantly useful and falls off less later game
1x Receptionist 1 - Can be EXCELLENT against Justice Passive and generally useful. Fantastic T2 as Player 2 and is great as a 4/6/6

**Tested DT's**
Great - Angel of Death, Omega Flowey
Good - Spamton Neo
Okay - The Heroine
Hit or Miss - Chara

**Not-Tested DT'S**
Potentially Good - Soulless Kris
Very hit or miss - Frisk, Clover, Ultimathrash (just run Papyrus lol)
No - The Vessel, Kris, Zenith Martlet, Giga Queen, Barrier

Speak of, Chujin Tombstone 7x, seems cool right? 

You can play Ctomb for Least untill you run out of paintings, then play it for Highest, get your non-gen Ctomb back, and then play that one for Highest, and get your other non-gen back, and you have now replayed 21 cards of your deck.

Sometimes you just got to say "well, I need healing/value RIGHT NOW, so highest." and that's fine, if you can play painting post Ctomb, do it.

GEN GIFTS IS LEGIT FINE! Like, actually though, but it can totally fucking ruin some turns with self lock, so look out for that, and keep track of numbers!

decks.notes:
<#1352018837054296124>
Made by Bugrofun (I'm back baby!)
From Diamond 4 to Legend with  12 winstreak until Master 2, but much rougher from here. Still huge 35-11 winrate overall.
After getting stuck on spider-stall-patience vs all those greedy midrange/control decks with skris/gigaqueen/snoelle/etc. I thought that i want something that can really punish doing nothing on the turns 1-3. And then i remembered about Abstract Art + Lamp + Monsterkid combo, which is giving you one of the most dangerous t3 boards possible. As it turns out, it's still as dangerous as it was, if not more.

Deck code:


Your main goal on this deck is take over the board and keep it yours at all costs. Your monsters are hard to remove and spawn more monsters, even 1/1 tokens can potentially be lethal for opponent, your health is a resource, all for breaking face and maintaining threat. You're using lamps to make sticky bodies, monster kids and grillby to buff those and trash rudinn, call of the grave and sometimes faded memory - to get more monster kids. MUCH more. My record is 13 (thirteen) on Martlet.

Martlet is here as finisher and theoretically replaceable by kris or Omega, but i wouldn't advice those. Keep in mind that dusts are triggering in order from dustpile, so if you have lamps first - you will get buffed snow frisks. usually you want your Trash Rudinns to give you Monster Kids, so you can use Faded memory to remove lamps if situation allows. Also faded memory can be used to reach lethal as 2G +4/+4 across the board, or as extra haste token.

Susie and Ceroba here as comeback and winmore plans respectively. Susie sucks when you're ahead already, but can really help to fight back from behind, and Ceroba is just good sometimes. Both can be replaced by 3rd copies of other cards of by other aggro options if you want (but keep in mind that you don't want Trash Rudinn to give you Temmie).

Screen of deck and current placement below:

Side note: i hate infinitlet as much as you are (although it's not a strong deck imo), but i do think that martlet is really cool in decks like that one

Almost forgot match history. Most of my loses were in master 1, but it still should be above 70% in onyx+. Some of those were due to bs (bravery getting exactly redbird into my first and last monsterkid), some due to my missplays, 1-2 - due to reaaally bad starting hand. Also plant justice is a really bad matchup due to it being "25 really cheap haste/removals" type of deck.

decks.author: truedregun
decks.notes:
s105 justice deck
played to legend from master, 22-4 (roughly 84% wr)
Some people were confused about how to win with the last deck, so I'm here to provide predicted sources of skill issue:
- Getting understatted cards that don't work with your deck from Mo
- Getting only common packs from Undercardio
- Getting bad cards from skris (example: 7g 12/11)
- Not drawing your 2 card combos
- Not drawing the DT in a timely manner
Here's what you should be doing instead:
- Getting cheap well statted cards with slightly relevant effects from Mo
- Getting at *least* 3 final packs from Undercardio
- Getting good cards from skris (example: Mane Ax)

decks.notes:
Silent-Ambush
The final version of this deck was played from about onix 4 to legend (top 9!)
4-29 

Ok, not to be XCriwn ⁠maxhpness desc here, so at the end there's a tldr, but i do recommend you read everything as the deck has some iffy situations you can get out of.

mulling out certain cards like:
if you have both figures, mull one
mull any ambush drop
One of the assaults 
if you think you REALLY need ambush, hard mull for it

Going second is based, in most situations you want to drop ambush and next turn play Action Figures on Arcade Samurai or The Warrior. the stats usually overwhelm the oppt and they die. ye.

Going first isn't what this deck prefers BUT if your oppt does nothing, you can pass and burn Dream for one extra turn for them to waste their moment playing any big drop, as ambush shits on that and you have something left over.

Recruit is a big part of making this deck useful. Picking drops that go with cards in the deck already like Trash Ball and Action Figures. Any 6 ATK drop and overheat/rage.

If you need to use both of your ambushes, do it. Take your oppt to Skris if you end up burning both of them (not like overdraw, but using them well) Skris gives this deck a late game, just push for recruit drops that give tokens like Organ Piano, Stalagmite, and Police Blook.

This deck struggles with zoo. use your overheats at the most devastating moments. ambush the most problematic thing and move on. Get Big Mouth, Pancakes, MTT Fountain, cards that heal or don't like zoo from recruit. other than that this deck kinda doesn't like charge spam but you outpace it if you're smart enough so don't worry about it.

TL, DR: Ambush. Play action figures on a 6/7/8 (Arcade) or  (The Warrior) 6/7/9, win game by clicking face, minecart does 3 face sometimes, USE YOUR RECRUITS WELL., and if that doesnt' work take them to skris and call them cring

decks.author: ivnb92
decks.notes:
skt-lib-dt
Played to Legend (5 of the games are on onyx alt thanks improved matchmaking quality!)

18-2 but full disclosure I played the last 5 games like ass cause I just wanted to post a deck <:hue:1137287556757721178>

-------------------------------------------------------------

- **Wah! This looks like a fast deck! What do I do against Dunebud!!**

Dunebud is a knowledge check. The deck's weakness is that all cards in it feed into Dunebud, meaning it's often forced to use a Dunebud for removing basically any board. Play this matchup slower, count their gold, and focus on attrition - after all 3 (most of the time, 2) Dunebuds die and you're not a few crystal shards away from losing the deck is all but dead. Keep that in mind and do not greed your cards, even payoff ones like Cyberdly and Clover. Just kill Dunebuds instead.

- **I added (insert dogshit tribe monster here) because Cyberdly!**

Put that away. The only acceptable inclusion here on that basis is Temmie (no, not Temmie Egg). Cyberdly commonly gives 2-3 Feather Storms here and that is absolutely fine. Additionally, Maus Hole, Mo and Reflection very often give you tribe monsters.

- **I mill my cards sometimes!**

And that is fine. Also, you can use Mo on two hand slots in a pinch. Do be aware of the fact he isn't OP anymore though (if you saw me saying he is, my opinion fluctuates on that fact).

- **I don't have Clover!**

The deck also works just as well with The Heroine and marginally less well with Ceroba Ketsukane.

- **Parsnik is worse Ceroba!**

Parsnik costing less means it's more easily playable into TNT Man lethals as well as in earlygame. It is also a Plant.

- **Rorrim is bad!**

Rorrim provides a 2-layered taunt wall for the rare random ahh chargespam, sets up TNT Man procs lategame, has Dealmaker synergy due to not summoning a TOKEN, and is underrated in general.

- **Alphys!!!**

No.

- **Chujin!!!**

No.

It is the uninteractive deck once more, this time featuring dumb broken cards i don't like (mo and clover) 

Your gameplan is to forcefully fill the board and punish the opponent hard for any monsters they left alive during their turn. Note that this does take some positioning and thinking ahead as well as good trading. At any given time half of your hand is probably a swing button so you can be really greedy with skip turns and sacking hp for tempo 

Some specifics: 
Snoelle is purely here because it's the cheapest paralyze and the haste is nice, I have only gone for snowgrave once and didn't even get to use it. 
Use Alphys proactively, on an empty board it's a merchire on command. Also yeah it does disrupt cyberd but no it doesn't matter, you only play it for 2 or even 1 feather storm anyways.
Mo is mo, its really good at pressing your advantage further
You do not get 1 turn final rounds with this deck, Clover is primarily just a fuck you semitaunt with burst attached here 
Pkris is your wincondition

This has some rough matchups but all are very doable. Whimsa dt in particular is terrifying tho, avoid it like the plague.

4/10 braincells required

decks.author: keith_ps
decks.notes:
balloon bravery
17-3 (85%) in legend

main combo: balloon on an empty hand to get net +2 gold with a 4/4 haste attached (remember that balloon is effectively 5G! or 6 with inflation, that cost me my last game)

generally mulligan for a playable opening hand, definitely keep deflated mascots as they are really good early (vomit them ASAP and protect them for max value) and really bad late (to the point of being accel fodder). keep in mind that maus hole guarantees a 3G balloon turn 3 via bravery passive

this deck plays as slow zoo / fast midrange. against faster decks where you've won on board, don't be afraid to facerace. but also you have almost no healing (except 3 hp from laser topper) so if you're only neutral on board then just defend until balloon. especially on balloon turn you want to explode for as much tempo as possible; keep in mind that a lot of bravery spells require a monster on board or a card in hand, so make sure to manage your hand so you don't brick yourself (i.e. keep a balloon and another monster in preparation for next turn so you can use a spell drawn on that monster)

this deck generally has really good matchups

but it can suffer into specifically hyperaggro like snails (one loss) and control with heavy antizoo and finishing power (two losses). people have started to run martlet as skris tech; don't be afraid to play very hard around martlet if you notice your opponent going for it because 0 cost martlet will be able to beat your skris endgame. also i haven't faced it in this match history but im pretty sure cjester is a bad matchup as well, so pray you avoid that deck lol

> None of these cards are in the bible
\- Sktima22

Played in legend, 18-2.
Lost to mid kindness buffs with kris highroll and a worse build that did not include Scissor Dancer.

Yes, this deck has Scissor Dancer. It also has Icemeter but you probably know that already.

Your earlygame heavily relies on matchup knowledge and how much of an asshole you are. Sometimes it's better to start off with Trader Temmie, Maus Hole, or, of course, Icemeter t1-t2. Sometimes it's better to defend, wait for their play, then do a big swing with quizbreaker/icemeter combo.

Maus Hole can give you spells. If it doesn't give you spells, it will probably still give you something reactive. Notably, you **can** take Trophy in this deck if you don't have better stuff to do and/or want more stats, it usually won't fuck up your curve. Inflation/Icemeter is also very scary.

Quizbreaker is self-explanatory and no, it doesn't make this deck cringe, because here it's used to make cool shit.

Head Hathy (and Caged Kings) give you 1G spells to the deck. If you draw Blitz Quiz or Snailmobile late, remember that.

Icemeter is the star of the show and a genuinely good card. You CAN play it alongside a single Blitz Quiz and just have a 3/4/4 Taunt earlygame. That's not bad at all.

Noelle is broken, next card.

Trader Temmie fuels SKris at a very funny speed and in general just synergizes perfectly with everything here.

Snailmobile is the most based draw in the game.

Snowdin Sign and Caged Kings are your proactive mid drops. Caged Kings *can* feel a bit too slow, but I think a lot of matchups would get worse without that extra push. You can get a third Maus Hole and a Mo if you wanna full commit to funny SKris, but I wouldn't.

Scissor Dancer is your answer to random kindness shit, random armors, darkspawn, big bob, and generally annoying fuckers. This card is sad even under ideal conditions... 4 ATK when?

Asgore is cool as fuck and genuinely good here. He also kills shamblings.

decks.author: pkmoritz
decks.notes:
Arcade Machine TAlphys:

With Kris and Ambition buffed, I really wanted to try building this. Here is what I came up with. It's obviously inconsistent and feels a bit like a meme but I think its really fun. Feel free to give any advice on how to improve this. Ik I'm not the greatest deck builder.

Anyways, how to Play:
The star of the show is Arcade Machine. With both patience and ambition it is surprisingly doable to get it up. If possible, use it while Kris is in hand to make Kris have cost reduction. Don't be to greedy with it. Even with the perfect cards in hand for Arcade Machine it can still be more important to react to your opponent first if they pressure you. By turn 10 you usually should have played at least one arcade machine but it doesnt have to be much earlier. 

For the endgame there is snowgrave and teacher alphys + ice replica. If possible try and use your snoelle early. You can get snowgrave up quite quickly because of patience and Ambition. If the opponent prevents 2 of your lost souls/fox head from entering your dustpile, consider killing snoelle to still get 6 chalks if its a slower game.

You only have one oasis, use it wisely. Since Arcade Machine lets you vomit out your entire hand quickly, you almost always want to use it as a handfill once your hand is pretty small.

Melt is crazy good. Its super strong with cybershelf and dancer. It's also really nice with arcade machine. Since arcade machine will make it cost 0 you can gain some board control while playing arcade machine.

This deck does require some brain cells to play. You should constantly think about rarities. Things like tiny tornado and especially cybershelf give you some control over the rarities in your hand.

decks.author: xcriwn
decks.notes:
Gachapon Gaming Show

Lanino and Elnina go on honeymoon to Tenna's marvelous Arcade!

Hard Mull for arcade machine, if you get it early you'll never regret it

TLDR: play for arcade machine when in hand, gachapon dt and skris are finishers

How to play:
When arcade machine is in hand: try really hard to play only the rarities you have dupes of in your hand, plan for the next turns and take into account the gachapon rarity and dream blocking one draw!
When arcade machine isn't in hand: play normally, don't take rarities into account too much, you don't want to play badly just for the chance of drawing the arcade machine.

On select occasions, you will have a chance to plan for arcade activation turn 5. When this happens, you gotta remember that gacha gives you common, if you already have common you dont have to play the ball! even overdraw if you have the perfect hand but need just a bit more money, arcade early game on full hand is incredible value!

Late game finishers are skris and gachapon legendary and dt rarity.
Other tips:
Don't greed arcade, if you can play it even on 3 cards, it's worth it.
If arcade botdecked, focus on surviving rather than attacking, once you draw it and activate it, you can switch gears. Asdree is also here to allow for easier switching of gears.

Explodes to:
not caring for arcade and letting it brick you
being so bricked early game you have no plays (only happened once) (i added weather duo since then and that wasnt an issue anymore)

Enjoy the arcade games!

Credit to @nightfalcon for helping me with ideas!

decks.author: jaimeeznuts
decks.notes:
hanging-rat-dt
17-3 played in A- to S+

omg rare jaimeepost

guide: mulligan for temmie/hanging plug/tpainting/kris. Obviously change this up depending on the matchup, like against justice temmie will be a lot weaker
Hanging plug can be used on just about ANYTHING that's not johnny maus hole or maus hole drop (and obvious stuff like grillby/ncg/zootsei hit by lostphys). It is really good to greed for hanging plug ncg so if you have an ncg hold out for that combo (unless necessary). On the contrary...do not greed tpainting. Tpainting is still good even with -2 or even just -1 cost. a 3/3/5 haste is still good. Also allows it to be hit by Hanging Plug.

Hanging plug and temmie particularly combo very well cause you can get a ton of face damage alongside decent stats for super cheap (this is even better with NCG). Ngl i would also just avoid hanging plugging a lost soul cause you have limited plugs and resetting charge/haste/trans is much better than simply getting a 3/3.

Gachapon is just good handfill, draw is a bit too slow for this deck but it can work. Reinforcement feels like bait but it could mayyybe work if you try hard enough.

Used to use a version that ran 3x Lamp over Flowey and Temmie...that version was still good but this version is a major improvement.

Your current flex slots are zootsei/ramb, those two just kind of felt Fine in gameplay imo. Kris can be subbed out for either raw Ultima, Clover, Titan, or, actually surprisingly, OF. You almost always have a board with this deck so OFing isn't that hard...though Titan/Ultima is likely better.

snoring 4patience
at least ~80% wr over at least 40 games, but the last 20 games are with this specific list (i think), going 18-2, played to top 1

this deck sort of ship-of-theseus'd itself into an entirely different deck over time so it's worth a new post

most of the deck is just typical midrange gamesense and change-of-winds management; do not be afraid to use date alphys for a good tempo trade because she is here for being good, not just for snoring/soul cage combos. generally try to send your topend plus a frozen energy to your botdeck so you have a lategame; also note that drawing your 1 costs with change-of-winds is generally optimal (unless you need to draw your dusty chest / cold winter) because they get less value from being redrawn and are best early

if all goes well, you will draw nearly your entire deck by turn 10 or so. if you're facing a deck that goes wide, definitely try to set up cold winter bullshit to insta-win early, otherwise it's fine to just cash it in for a cycle

note for budget players: the seam / vessel topend is actually kinda optional, you can try running whatever as long as it can close out games efficiently

decks.author: mark_callisto
decks.notes:
Midrange Widow DT

Played to early season top 1 
33/2, aka 93% wr, losses due to terrible play vs wuvea twice, winrate slightly inflated because I should have lost 2 more games but got really lucky.

Yeah so who would have guessed that the artifact that gives you a near infinite supply of 1g deal 2’s and occasional free 4 drops that synergises with some of the best tempo cards in the game is pretty good in midrange? I for one am flabbergasted

Gameplan: you have a lot of sustainability earlygame with the combination of spider donut, lost paps and dt extra life, meaning you get a lot of time to get a ton of value from your artifact by just trading, forcing at worst a neutral position in the midgame. From there you can just go face at all opportunities and simply use your burst options to finish out the game

Some notes: 
Zoot kris kinda just wins the earlygame a lot of the time if you get it down, if you need to its completely fine to mill just to get sharpshoot in hand
Its also completely fine to use libloox as a big vulkin, unless you are against control you often dont need the extra face damage 
Pretty sure this is already widely known at this point but royal dt, even if you only have 14 in dustpile, is amazing

This deck runs 1x first starwalker and gets away with it

Oh also credit to Melody for inspiration

4/10 Braincells required

this did beat <#1448073653680869567> netdeckers a lot because seedling eventually fucks up their cold winter and the MOMENT they cannot cw you off the board it is straight up over. my advice vs that is to not be TOO afraid of rockman bullseye. they have fucking **vessel **as their dt, and you scale. as long as they're stuck trading you will eventually just win.

as usual for seedlings deck, tracking your passive procs is IMPERATIVE and if you frequently forget which lost soul it is, your winrate will show. this is because you can insta-trigger a lot of lost soul dusts with seedlings cause they go first.

speaking of lost souls, do not play a green flower if you're getting a lost soul summoned in anyway and don't wanna trigger its dust. basically, do not waste an 1/1. unless you want handspace.

pillar is not here just for hanging buffs use it aggressively

decks.notes:
Cyber Baloon Zoo:

I find new Cyber Baloon very interesting and wanted to build around it. Besides Baloon this is very generic Bravery Zoo. I don't really play much Bravery so if anyone has improvment suggestions feel free to share. 

How to play:
Great Openers are Lamp and Maus. You want to be very mindful of your generated monsters. Keep parachutism turns and bravery passive turns in mind as those are consistent ways to get generated monsters. Usually its smart to keep some generated monsters in hand for easier baloon value and greater overgrowths. Especially green flowers in hand make cyberbaloon a lot less clunky. Make sure to get value out of baloon, if you only pay 2G but also only deal 2 DMG you have not achieved anything. Your recrutiment picks matter a lot so always be super mindful of the board state, gold amounts and your cards in hand when picking something

Gourmet Kindness, from A (Onyx II) rank to T rank (Legend)

The story shown is 16-3, the scribbled game wasnt with the deck. There were other matches tho and I think its like 23-5 or something like that



This deck is like a gourmetized burn deck or something because it has lots of burst while still being able to maintain some board. It doesn’t really counter DTEX cloning but I did win some matches against the deck, it mostly depends on the amount of oasis they get and when they get extractor, as well as your own draws ofc.

The gameplan is pretty simple which is just Deal Face DMG. Flying Heads and Mines Sign are good openers and Great Door helps with boards. Sometimes you will need to skip turns to get the right amount of G to kill your opponent (not necessarily skipping for RK) and I like doing that when the opponent locks themselves with low-mid stat monsters since they deal little damage.

decks.author: sktima
decks.notes:
19-1 from A to legend. The only loss was to me getting pizza, fucking up that turn, and then grossly miscounting my damage on the following one <:hue:1137287556757721178>

AKA hitler patience, aka sequel to <#1410210779596329052> 

Hard mulligan for dunebud. Your ideal opener is dunebud + replica. 

If they don't answer it, you basically win vs anything board-centric but you may get cheesed — if you're really afraid of that your anti cheese options include playing a bag or tall miner to force them to respond, having 2 ice shocks in hand, or using frozen energy to strengthen your board (in order of preference).

If they do answer it, look for a 2g spell off your change of winds — if you find it, you can immediately retaliate with dunebud + 2g spell from hand + cost-reduced 1g spell from topdeck.

That's it. Keep your sweet little bundle of cancer all over the board until you get enough chip damage and gold to simply burn people with gemstones/shards. Don't be afraid of playing miner/bag for tempo but keep track of how many you have left (replica helps).

The biggest skill factors are ordering (which can get complicated here especially since this is a very APMy deck) and hand management. A lot of long-term hand management.

Cooler cooler is your flex slot. Replace it as you will. I chose this card bc it's a budget 4th dunebud (this deck REALLY wants dunebud tho) and if drawn on t2 as p2 vs zoo, with fenergy it can trade into a 1 atk monster and make them wish they played league of legends instead of this game.

Remember that 2-3-4 bags can also output huge damage and it is more gold efficient than miner if you have the spells for it. This will come up rarely, but it will come up.

You can actually run this with no DT. Vessel is not that strong here, though it helps.

Wow okay yeah I HATE thorn ring i HATE dune bud I hate Pot Of Greed and finally, I HATE ICE SHOCK.

playing this deck vs. ring:
you are on an 8-9 turn timer. your opponent has no taunt monsters and will likely try to ice shock you early or late to slow you down, keep this in mind and don't play into it. get a full board and cast strengths as soon/wisely as possible, and click face. chances are you will win due to raw damage, and try to keep your board full.

playing this VS PV or Justice
you may not want to play monsters right away. play them maybe when you have access to a full board and no sooner, the rest of the game will depend on what kind of deck they are playing, so be an undercards player.

Playing this VS everything else
keep your board full, force them to trade, if you get behind, play for skris.

decks.author: mark_callisto
decks.notes:
Titan Fuzzy Perseverance

17/3, aka 85% wr, all losses due to missplays, played to top 10

This deck is being posted out of spite, I overhauled this shit like 6 times to get to this version and I want to get it out of my system, that being said: very fun deck

The star of the show is fuzzy, its incredibly versatile earlygame if you have the small spells to fuel it and it can get honestly very stupid amounts of value in the midgame if its allowed to attack multiple times per turn or just grow to become like a 2/7/7, tall miner fuzzy can genuinely sweep just about any board.

The rest of the deck is just here to take advantage of the metric shitload of small ping you have and to fuel skris. Fountain usually doesn't actually come into play that much in most matchups, it's just here to break control 

Ring matchup is even, if you manage to get fuzzy up early they basically cant stop it and you have some other tools as well like a bunch of deal 2, game bomb, trolley problem and tall miner but that matchup is so volatile that it usually just comes down to who gets scammed by draw rng and who doesnt

because its funny: lock matchup is 90/10, yes i lost in the history but thats because i completely lost the plot in that match, all other variants of this deck went undefeated

Sorry for forgetting before, credit to Melody for opop concept

7.5/10 braincells required, if you mess up once you explode
